chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: Hello guys, my name is Nitish and welcome to my YouTube channel. So we are slowly moving forward in our deep learning course and in the last two videos we have studied about Multilayer Perceptron. In the first video we saw the intuition of Multilayer Perceptron and in the last video I told you its notation

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: that how you name its weights and biases so that there is no confusion in the future. So I will tell you what is the whole flow. We are slowly moving towards a very important topic which is called Backpropagation.

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: So backpropagation is the algorithm by which you train neural networks. And it is said that it is a little difficult to understand backpropagation. So I have planned that before understanding backpropagation we will simplify things a lot.

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: So when you will read backpropagation then you will realize that there are two things one is forward propagation. Forward propagation is where your data i.e. your input

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: moves forward in your neural network through each layer. And backpropagation is when you come to the back side and update the weights through which training happens.

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: So I thought that before reading backpropagation you should have a very good idea that how forward propagation works. So in this video I will teach you forward propagation. Or you can say in simple words

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: that if you have an input and if there are some weights in your neural network then how do you use those weights and inputs to make predictions. I want to show you this.

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: And you will see that this whole thing handles linear algebra so beautifully. No matter how big the architecture is it does not mean that at the end you just have to do dot products of matrices.

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: So I just wanted to give you this overview video so that's why I have shot today's video. I would like to discuss one more thing with you. You must have noticed in all the courses that generally people after reading backpropagation

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: move towards real world data sets and read it like examples. But I am going to do something different in this course. Before backpropagation I will use Keras library

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: and train neural network on some real world data sets. I know we have not studied training yet but we do not have to write its code ourselves. Keras will handle behind the scenes. So I just want to give you a perspective

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: that how things work. Once you see it when you go to read backpropagation you will understand it better. And then we will do some projects after that.

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: So my plan is that in today's video we will learn about forward propagation or you can say how a neural network does prediction. And then in the next 2-3 videos

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: we will work on some data sets with Keras library. So that's it for the introduction. Let's start the video. So guys let's learn in this video

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: that how a neural network does prediction. So neural network is a complex architecture where you can put any number of nodes or

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: add any number of layers. Our goal will be that the prediction that comes out of such a complex architecture should be learned in a simplified way.

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: The good thing is that linear algebra handles everything behind the scenes. You just have to understand the flow.

chunks.title: Forward Propagation | How a neural network predicts output
chunks.text: So on the screen there is a neural network where this is the architecture. There are 2 hidden layers and 1 output layer.

chunks.text: I have this data. I have 4 input columns in the data. CGPA, IQ, 10th marks, 12th marks and the output column is placed.

So I will tell you what is the whole flow. We are slowly moving towards a very important topic which is called Backpropagation. So backpropagation is the algorithm by which you train neural networks. And it is said that it is a little difficult to understand backpropagation. So I have planned that before understanding backpropagation we will simplify things a lot. So when you will read backpropagation then you will realize that there are two things one is forward propagation. Forward propagation is where your data i.e. your input moves forward in your neural network through each layer. And backpropagation is when you come to the back side and update the weights through which training happens. So I thought that before reading backpropagation you should have a very good idea that how forward propagation works. So in this video I will teach you forward propagation. Or you can say in simple words that if you have an input and if there are some weights in your neural network then how do you use those weights and inputs to make predictions. I want to show you this. And you will see that this whole thing handles linear algebra so beautifully. No matter how big the architecture is it does not mean that at the end you just have to do dot products of matrices.
